headers.comment: Firebase applies the LAST matching rule per header key, so the broad no-cache default comes first and the immutable overrides for hashed assets come after. HTML at clean URLs (/, /about) only matches this rule, so it revalidates every load and a redeploy reaches users immediately.

Hashed Astro assets and content-hashed photos never change under a given name — cache hard so repeat visits don't re-download them (matters for the Spark daily transfer limit). These come AFTER ** so they win.
